title: Multipolar exchange in a many-body homonuclear mixture of atoms in different internal states
authors: M. Bulakhov, A. S. Peletminskii, Yu. V. Slyusarenko
abstract: We develop a general method for constructing the many-body Hamiltonian of pairwise interactions describing homonuclear mixtures of atoms occupying states with different total angular momenta or other quantum numbers. The advantage of the irreducible spherical tensor operator formalism is demonstrated: these operators give the Hamiltonian an explicit physical structure, account for all scattering channels, and include multipolar exchange interactions. The latter correspond to the exchange of both angular-momentum projections and the total angular momentum. Particular realizations of the general Hamiltonian, widely used in the physics of ultracold gases, are also analyzed. The resulting Hamiltonian provides a universal framework for investigating a broad range of quantum many-body phenomena in bosonic and fermionic atomic gases.
summary: This paper introduces a generalized many-body Hamiltonian for ultracold atomic mixtures with different internal angular momentum states. By using spherical tensor operators, the authors provide a framework that captures all possible multipolar exchange processes. This approach offers a more universal and physically intuitive description than previous methods for studying high-spin quantum gases.
main_problem: The lack of a consistent theoretical framework to describe many-body interactions in homonuclear mixtures where atoms occupy internal states with different total angular momenta or other quantum numbers.
main_result: The development of a universal many-body Hamiltonian using irreducible spherical tensor operators that explicitly accounts for both the exchange of angular-momentum projections and the exchange of total angular momentum.
method: The authors utilize the irreducible spherical tensor operator formalism, applying the Wigner-Eckart theorem, Clebsch-Gordan coefficients, and 6j symbols to decompose the interaction into multipolar exchange terms.
model_or_system: A many-body system of interacting, indistinguishable, homonuclear ultracold bosonic and fermionic atoms in different internal quantum states, including specific cases like Helium, Strontium, Ytterbium, Erbium, and Dysprosium.
key_observables: Interaction amplitudes (U_12^F), scattering channels (F), and multipole components (scalar, dipole, quadrupole, etc.).
important_parameters: Total angular momentum (f), its projection (m), s-wave scattering regime, and weak external magnetic fields.
main_assumptions: The system is in the regime of weak external magnetic fields and low-energy s-wave scattering; atoms are sufficiently distant to allow for a multipole expansion of the electromagnetic potential.
figures_summary: Figure 1 uses Feynman diagrams to represent the effective exchange of virtual particles, specifically a virtual scalar particle and a virtual photon without total angular momentum transfer.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the physical necessity of the new Hamiltonian, demonstrates the mathematical advantages of the spherical tensor approach over SU(N) frameworks, reproduces known results for equal angular momentum (Sec. 3), and presents new results for mixtures with different angular momenta (Sec. 4).
why_interesting: It provides a mathematically robust and physically transparent tool for studying high-spin ultracold gases and multicomponent lattice models, moving beyond standard SU(N) models to include complex multipolar exchange.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Feynman diagrams of the interaction terms in the Hamiltonian, represented as an effective exchange of: (a) a virtual scalar particle, (b) a virtual photon without total angular momentum transfer, and (c) a virtual photon with total angular momentum transfer.
